mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held above 4.0% signals a restrictive monetary stance that keeps discount rates elevated and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing a persistent drag on S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or mean forward return during FEDFUNDS>4.0% periods is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals an inflection into labor market deterioration that historically precedes broader earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: In periods where the 0.3pp trigger fires, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect size is not statistically distinct from unconditional base rate (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS growth combined with a still-elevated FEDFUNDS rate indicates the Fed is maintaining restrictive policy into a weakening labor market, a joint condition historically associated with negative equity forward returns because policy easing lags the labor deterioration, over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS 159398k, FEDFUNDS 4.33% as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with labor slowdown
failure_condition: Joint-condition triggered periods show forward 20d SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional distribution (p>0.05), or positive returns occur in >55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a deceleration in INDPRO growth signals an approaching industrial slowdown that markets underprice, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; in early-cycle recoveries the same deceleration is noise and has no reliable directional effect.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO deceleration signal and forward 60d returns is below 0.03 conditional on late-cycle regime classification, or effect reverses sign across two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects real-time consumer spending momentum that feeds directly into near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing a positive short-horizon (5-day) drift in S&P 500 returns whenever retail sales growth accelerates, as an always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS MoM growth and forward 5d SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign of correlation flips across two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST below 1,300 thousand units signals housing sector contraction that historically leads broader demand weakness with a lag, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month once the threshold is breached.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1501.0 thousand units as of 2025-02
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns following HOUST threshold breaches are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously high, the joint condition signals sustained restrictive real rates that suppress risk appetite and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; this interaction effect is stronger than either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 319.615 and FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: Triggered joint-condition periods yield forward 60d SPX returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p>0.05), or positive returns occur in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, a monthly PAYEMS print below the trailing 6-month average signals labor market fragility that traders react to swiftly, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the very short 5-day horizon; the same weak print in an easing regime is interpreted as dovish and produces no reliable negative reaction.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change below trailing 6-month average while FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS 159398k as of 2025-03, FEDFUNDS 4.33% as of 2025-03
failure_condition: During tightening-regime PAYEMS weak-print events, forward 5d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.
